revision.comment: CRS is pretty much implied by map, so it's pretty wrong to say there is none. Update list of WMA bodies.
revision.text: The globe: parameter specifies the [[planet]], [[dwarf planet]], [[asteroid]], or [[natural satellite]] upon which the coordinates reside. Apart from ''[[earth]]'' (the default), recognized values are: ''[[Mercury (planet)|mercury]]'', ''[[venus]]'', ''[[moon]]'', ''[[mars]]'', ''[[Phobos (moon)|phobos]]'', ''[[Deimos (moon)|deimos]]'', ''[[Ceres (dwarf planet)|ceres]]'', ''[[4 Vesta|vesta]]'', ''[[jupiter]]'', ''[[Ganymede (moon)|ganymede]]'', ''[[Callisto (moon)|callisto]]'', ''[[Io (moon)|io]]'', ''[[Europa (moon)|europa]]'', ''[[Mimas (moon)|mimas]]'', ''[[enceladus]]'', ''[[Tethys (moon)|tethys]]'', ''[[Dione (moon)|dione]]'', ''[[Rhea (moon)|rhea]]'', ''[[Titan (moon)|titan]]'', ''[[Hyperion (moon)|hyperion]]'', ''[[Iapetus (moon)|iapetus]]'', ''[[Phoebe (moon)|phoebe]]'', ''[[Miranda (moon)|miranda]]'', ''[[Ariel (moon)|ariel]]'', ''[[Umbriel (moon)|umbriel]]'', ''[[Titania (moon)|titania]]'', ''[[Oberon (moon)|oberon]]'', ''[[Triton (moon)|triton]]'', ''[[pluto]]'', and ''[[Charon (moon)|charon]]''. {| class="wikitable sortable" |+ Samples ! Subject ! G ! Markup ! Result |- | [[Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The pop-out WikiMiniAtlas system provides limited mapping for Moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, Io, and Titan, {{as of|2021|02|lc=1}}. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. [[Category:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]]

revision.comment: CRS is pretty much implied by map, so it's pretty wrong to say there is none. Update list of WMA bodies.
revision.text: The globe: parameter specifies the [[planet]], [[dwarf planet]], [[asteroid]], or [[natural satellite]] upon which the coordinates reside. Apart from ''[[earth]]'' (the default), recognized values are: ''[[Mercury (planet)|mercury]]'', ''[[venus]]'', ''[[moon]]'', ''[[mars]]'', ''[[Phobos (moon)|phobos]]'', ''[[Deimos (moon)|deimos]]'', ''[[Ceres (dwarf planet)|ceres]]'', ''[[4 Vesta|vesta]]'', ''[[jupiter]]'', ''[[Ganymede (moon)|ganymede]]'', ''[[Callisto (moon)|callisto]]'', ''[[Io (moon)|io]]'', ''[[Europa (moon)|europa]]'', ''[[Mimas (moon)|mimas]]'', ''[[enceladus]]'', ''[[Tethys (moon)|tethys]]'', ''[[Dione (moon)|dione]]'', ''[[Rhea (moon)|rhea]]'', ''[[Titan (moon)|titan]]'', ''[[Hyperion (moon)|hyperion]]'', ''[[Iapetus (moon)|iapetus]]'', ''[[Phoebe (moon)|phoebe]]'', ''[[Miranda (moon)|miranda]]'', ''[[Ariel (moon)|ariel]]'', ''[[Umbriel (moon)|umbriel]]'', ''[[Titania (moon)|titania]]'', ''[[Oberon (moon)|oberon]]'', ''[[Triton (moon)|triton]]'', ''[[pluto]]'', and ''[[Charon (moon)|charon]]''. {| class="wikitable sortable" |+ Samples ! Subject ! G ! Markup ! Result |- | [[Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The pop-out WikiMiniAtlas system provides limited mapping for Moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, Io, and Titan, {{as of|2021|02|lc=1}}. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. [[Category:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]]

Result |- | [[Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[:en:Apollo 11|Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The pop-out WikiMiniAtlas system provides limited mapping for Moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, Io, and Titan, {{as of|2021|02|lc=1}}. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. Result |- | [[Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[:en:Apollo 11|Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The pop-out WikiMiniAtlas system provides limited mapping for Moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, Io, and Titan, {{as of|2021|02|lc=1}}. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. [[Category:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]]

revision.text: The globe: parameter specifies the [[planet]], [[:en:Dwarf planet|Dwarf planet]], [[:en:Asteroid|Asteroid]], or [[natural satellite]] upon which the coordinates reside. Apart from ''[[:en:Earth|Earth]]'' (the default), recognized values are: ''[[Mercury (planet)|mercury]]'', ''[[venus]]'', ''[[moon]]'', ''[[:en:Mars|Mars]]'', ''[[Phobos (moon)|phobos]]'', ''[[:en:Deimos (moon)|deimos]]'', ''[[:en:Ceres (dwarf planet)|ceres]]'', ''[[:en:4 Vesta|vesta]]'', ''[[:en:Jupiter|Jupiter]]'', ''[[:en:Ganymede (moon)|ganymede]]'', ''[[:en:Callisto (moon)|callisto]]'', ''[[:en:Io (moon)|io]]'', ''[[:en:Europa (moon)|europa]]'', ''[[Mimas (moon)|mimas]]'', ''[[:en:Enceladus|Enceladus]]'', ''[[Tethys (moon)|tethys]]'', ''[[:en:Dione (moon)|dione]]'', ''[[Rhea (moon)|rhea]]'', ''[[Titan (moon)|titan]]'', ''[[:en:Hyperion (moon)|hyperion]]'', ''[[:en:Iapetus (moon)|iapetus]]'', ''[[Phoebe (moon)|phoebe]]'', ''[[Miranda (moon)|miranda]]'', ''[[:en:Ariel (moon)|ariel]]'', ''[[Umbriel (moon)|umbriel]]'', ''[[Titania (moon)|titania]]'', ''[[Oberon (moon)|oberon]]'', ''[[Triton (moon)|triton]]'', ''[[pluto]]'', and ''[[:en:Charon (moon)|charon]]''. {| class="wikitable sortable" |+ Samples ! Subject ! G ! Markup ! Result |- | [[:en:Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[:en:Apollo 11|Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The pop-out WikiMiniAtlas system provides limited mapping for Moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, Io, and Titan, {{as of|2021|02|lc=1}}. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. [[Category:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]]

Subject ! G ! Markup ! Result |- | [[:en:Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[:en:Apollo 11|Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. revision.text: The globe: parameter specifies the [[planet]], [[:en:Dwarf planet|Dwarf planet]], [[:en:Asteroid|Asteroid]], or [[:en:Natural satellite|Natural satellite]] upon which the coordinates reside. Apart from ''[[:en:Earth|Earth]]'' (the default), recognized values are: ''[[:en:Mercury (planet)|mercury]]'', ''[[venus]]'', ''[[:en:Moon|Moon]]'', ''[[:en:Mars|Mars]]'', ''[[Phobos (moon)|phobos]]'', ''[[:en:Deimos (moon)|deimos]]'', ''[[:en:Ceres (dwarf planet)|ceres]]'', ''[[:en:4 Vesta|vesta]]'', ''[[:en:Jupiter|Jupiter]]'', ''[[:en:Ganymede (moon)|ganymede]]'', ''[[:en:Callisto (moon)|callisto]]'', ''[[:en:Io (moon)|io]]'', ''[[:en:Europa (moon)|europa]]'', ''[[:en:Mimas (moon)|mimas]]'', ''[[:en:Enceladus|Enceladus]]'', ''[[Tethys (moon)|tethys]]'', ''[[:en:Dione (moon)|dione]]'', ''[[Rhea (moon)|rhea]]'', ''[[Titan (moon)|titan]]'', ''[[:en:Hyperion (moon)|hyperion]]'', ''[[:en:Iapetus (moon)|iapetus]]'', ''[[Phoebe (moon)|phoebe]]'', ''[[:en:Miranda (moon)|miranda]]'', ''[[:en:Ariel (moon)|ariel]]'', ''[[Umbriel (moon)|umbriel]]'', ''[[Titania (moon)|titania]]'', ''[[:en:Oberon (moon)|oberon]]'', ''[[Triton (moon)|triton]]'', ''[[pluto]]'', and ''[[:en:Charon (moon)|charon]]''. {| class="wikitable sortable" |+ Samples ! Subject ! G ! Markup ! Result |- | [[:en:Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[:en:Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[:en:Apollo 11|Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The pop-out WikiMiniAtlas system provides limited mapping for Moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, Io, and Titan, {{as of|2021|02|lc=1}}. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. [[Category:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: The globe: parameter specifies the [[:en:Planet|Planet]], [[:en:Dwarf planet|Dwarf planet]], [[:en:Asteroid|Asteroid]], or [[:en:Natural satellite|Natural satellite]] upon which the coordinates reside. Apart from ''[[:en:Earth|Earth]]'' (the default), recognized values are: ''[[:en:Mercury (planet)|mercury]]'', ''[[venus]]'', ''[[:en:Moon|Moon]]'', ''[[:en:Mars|Mars]]'', ''[[:en:Phobos (moon)|phobos]]'', ''[[:en:Deimos (moon)|deimos]]'', ''[[:en:Ceres (dwarf planet)|ceres]]'', ''[[:en:4 Vesta|vesta]]'', ''[[:en:Jupiter|Jupiter]]'', ''[[:en:Ganymede (moon)|ganymede]]'', ''[[:en:Callisto (moon)|callisto]]'', ''[[:en:Io (moon)|io]]'', ''[[:en:Europa (moon)|europa]]'', ''[[:en:Mimas (moon)|mimas]]'', ''[[:en:Enceladus|Enceladus]]'', ''[[Tethys (moon)|tethys]]'', ''[[:en:Dione (moon)|dione]]'', ''[[:en:Rhea (moon)|rhea]]'', ''[[Titan (moon)|titan]]'', ''[[:en:Hyperion (moon)|hyperion]]'', ''[[:en:Iapetus (moon)|iapetus]]'', ''[[:en:Phoebe (moon)|phoebe]]'', ''[[:en:Miranda (moon)|miranda]]'', ''[[:en:Ariel (moon)|ariel]]'', ''[[:en:Umbriel (moon)|umbriel]]'', ''[[Titania (moon)|titania]]'', ''[[:en:Oberon (moon)|oberon]]'', ''[[Triton (moon)|triton]]'', ''[[:en:Pluto|Pluto]]'', and ''[[:en:Charon (moon)|charon]]''. {| class="wikitable sortable" |+ Samples ! Subject ! G ! Markup ! Result |- | [[:en:Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[:en:Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[:en:Apollo 11|Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[:en:Venera 13|Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The pop-out WikiMiniAtlas system provides limited mapping for Moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, Io, and Titan, {{as of|2021|02|lc=1}}. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. [[Category:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]]

Result |- | [[:en:Kittu (crater)|Kittu Crater]] | ganymede | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} | {{coord|0.4|N|334.6|W|globe:ganymede}} |- | [[:en:Viking 2|Viking 2]] lander | mars | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} | {{coord|48.269|N|225.990|W|globe:mars}} |- | [[:en:Mozart (crater)|Mozart Crater]] | mercury | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} | {{coord|7.8|N|190.5|W|globe:mercury}} |- | [[:en:Apollo 11|Apollo 11]] lander | moon | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} | {{coord|0|40|26.69|N|23|28|22.69|E|globe:moon}} |- | [[Ksa (crater)|Ksa Crater]] | titan | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} | {{coord|14.0|N|65.4|W|globe:titan}} |- | [[:en:Venera 13|Venera 13]] lander | venus | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} | {{coord|7.5|S|303|E|globe:venus}} |- | [[:en:Stickney (crater)|Stickney Crater]] | phobos | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} | {{coord|1|N|49|W|globe:phobos}} |} Very rough mapping is provided on geohack for almost all supported globes. The pop-out WikiMiniAtlas system provides limited mapping for Moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, Io, and Titan, {{as of|2021|02|lc=1}}. The maps roughly implies a coordinate reference system, but does not clearly specify one (unlike Earth's WGS84). Since the template defaults to east longitude, the |W| direction must be specified for globes that measure longitude westward. For celestial coordinates, use {{tl|Sky}} instead. [[Category:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]]
